concept_seed.one_liner: An experimental amateur meteorology and storm-chasing community website with radar-green monospace UI, live doppler embeds, chase log entries, gear review pages, regional spotter forum, an event calendar of severe weather outlooks, photo gallery of supercells, and a callsign-driven member directory.

metadata.palette: radar green, black, amber alert orange

accepted_concept.site_goal: Provide a dense, dashboard-style hub where amateur storm chasers can monitor live doppler, file chase logs, review gear, coordinate spotter networks, and connect via callsign-based identity.
audience: ham radio operators with weather interest
accepted_concept.description: TORNADO ALLEY OPERATIONS NETWORK ('TAON-1') — an experimental utilitarian web app styled like a 1990s NOAA workstation crossed with a ham radio packet cluster. The entire UI runs in radar-green IBM Plex Mono on near-black (#050807), with amber alert orange used only for WATCH/WARNING states and clickable affordances. Layout is grid-heavy with thin 1px borders, ASCII separators, and live-updating numeric readouts. Every page header carries a 'PACKET' status line: UTC clock, current SPC outlook category, active chaser count, and last-strike timestamp. Multi-column information density is the point — sidebars, ticker bars, and inline mini-radars are everywhere. No hero images, no marketing fluff; instead, embedded GR2Analyst-style radar tiles, sparkline gauges, and tables of CAPE/Helicity/SRH values. Callsigns (e.g. 'KC5-WHIRL', 'N0-SCUD') are the primary identity throughout. The tone is field-operator: terse, abbreviation-heavy, with footer disclaimers about not interpreting outputs as official NWS guidance.
accepted_concept.motif: monospace dashboard with ASCII rules, radar sweeps, callsign tags, and amber WATCH/WARNING chips

pages.layout_pattern: four-quadrant operations console: top-left live CONUS doppler mosaic, top-right SPC convective outlook map with day-1/2/3 tabs, bottom-left ticker of recent member chase pings (callsign + lat/lon + time), bottom-right rolling LSR (Local Storm Reports) feed; persistent top status bar with UTC clock, sun/moon, and dewpoint readouts

tabular log archive: filter rail (year, state, EF-rating, member callsign, storm mode), main table with sortable columns (date, location, intercept time, storm type, max hail, peak wind, distance driven), expandable row reveals embedded GPS track, hodograph snapshot, and narrative entry

review database styled as a parts catalog: left nav by category (mobile mesonets, anemometers, hail guards, mesh radios, dashcams, laptops, lightning detectors), center grid of reviewed items with mini-spec readouts, right-rail comparison cart that holds up to four items for side-by-side spec diff

regional forum index: collapsible accordion of NWS County Warning Areas (CWAs) as forum boards, each row shows post count, last-callsign-active, and a tiny activity sparkline; threaded discussion view uses monospace with quoted reply ancestry indented with '>' marks

month grid where each day cell color-codes SPC categorical risk (MRGL/SLGT/ENH/MOD/HIGH) with amber escalation; clicking a day opens a side panel with hazard breakdown (tornado/wind/hail probabilities), participating chasers, and rendezvous waypoints

dense masonry of supercell photographs on black with thin amber borders; each tile carries an EXIF strip (lens, focal length, GPS, time, callsign of photographer) and storm metadata (mesocyclone tag, RFD slot, lowering type); filter chips for 'wall cloud', 'inflow band', 'mammatus', 'LP', 'HP', 'classic'
sections: masonry photo grid with EXIF strips

pages.layout_pattern: callsign-driven member directory: searchable table by callsign, real name (optional), home CWA, vehicle ID, certifications (SKYWARN, ARRL, HAM class), and last-active-on-net timestamp; profile detail view shows chase mileage total, storm-mode preferences, and rig schematic

message_intent: signal operational seriousness and field credibility
make dense real-time data legible at a glance
foreground callsign identity over real-name social patterns
communicate that this is community-run, not an official NWS service
interactions: gear comparison cart up to four items
photo lightbox with radar overlay toggle
accepted_concept.mobile_behavior: quadrants stack vertically into a single scrollable column; persistent packet status bar collapses to UTC clock + active chaser count; tables become horizontally scrollable with sticky callsign column; gear comparison cart becomes a bottom sheet

strengths: Tight fit with seed's 'experimental utilitarian dashboard' framing — four-quadrant ops console reads as a genuine NOAA-workstation pastiche, not generic template
Seven distinct, concrete pages with differentiated layout patterns (quadrant dashboard, tabular log archive, parts catalog, CWA accordion, risk-shaded calendar, masonry+EXIF, callsign roster)
Specific motif vocabulary: packet status bar, ASCII separators, '>' quote nesting, callsign tags like KC5-WHIRL, amber WATCH/WARNING chips — avoids vagueness
Interactions are useful and motivated: comparison cart up to four items, day-cell side panel, photo lightbox with radar overlay, callsign hover-card
Required text and disclaimers ('NOT AN OFFICIAL NWS PRODUCT') reinforce community-run framing realistically
Content model is domain-true: LSR pings, hodograph snapshots, CAPE/Helicity/SRH tables, CWAs
weaknesses: Tone is uniformly terse across all pages; could risk monotony without the longer-form variation candidate B introduces
Gear comparison and directory pages, while concrete, are the more conventional pieces of the set

strengths: Split-rail console + reading column is a genuinely experimental layout invention that still honors the dashboard palette
Distinct motif assets (hodograph spiral dividers, isobar contour pattern, ASCII compass roses, bracketed callsign tags) give the site a recognizable signature

Seven concrete pages with meaningfully different patterns (bulletin split, masonry+full reading view, test-rig two-column, CB-net roster with check-in queue, seasonal timeline, curated portfolio, guild ledger)
Adds storytelling depth (narrative field logs, briefer bios, photo portfolio) the seed allows without diluting domain credibility
Net check-in queue and 'tonight's net control callsign + frequency' is a sharp, realistic detail
weaknesses: Leans slightly away from the 'very high density' seed metadata by giving real estate to a reading column
Some page goals overlap conceptually with A (calendar/briefings, directory/guild) but execution is differentiated enough to stand
'EST. 2011' required text feels arbitrary versus A's more operational required strings

issues.message: Filter inputs (e.g. callsign text field, year/state/EF-rating checkbox groups) appear to lack programmatic labels — extractor flagged missing_form_label and missing_accessible_name.
Callsign search input lacks an associated label; extractor flagged missing_form_label / missing_accessible_name.
Large blank region (~37.9% sparsity, ~642k px area below the 12-row table) — the log table only renders one page worth of rows then leaves the lower viewport empty.
Three of eight gear cards in the mobile-mesonet grid have mismatched internal hierarchy/tag structure compared to the majority pattern (node-95, node-146, node-218).
The 12 storm-feature filter-chip / thumbnail glyphs show inconsistent size and underlying shape across the group.
Layout consistency score is 48 — calendar grid has 28 alignment errors out of 64 elements (row-bottom alignment and vertical-alignment groups).

repair_instructions: On /logs, the page currently renders only ~12 visible rows and then leaves a ~530px-tall blank band beneath the table. Either (a) increase the rows-per-page so the table fills the viewport down to the pagination control, or (b) move ancillary content (export controls, schema notes, last-modified strip, or a mini chart summarizing record counts by year/EF) into that blank region so the page reads as dense rather than truncated.

Add explicit <label> elements (or aria-label/aria-labelledby) to the form controls on /logs and /roster. Specifically: the callsign text input on /logs (placeholder 'e.g. KC5-WHIRL'), the callsign search input on /roster (placeholder 'search callsign / CWA / vehicle ID…'), and each filter checkbox group (Year, State/CWA, EF-Rating, Storm Mode) should have a programmatically associated label or aria-labelledby pointing at the section heading.

On /gear, normalize the internal structure of every card in the mobile-mesonet grid. The majority pattern is: icon block → <h3> title → body paragraph → spec rows → COMPARE button. Three cards (the ones flagged as node-95, node-146, node-218 — these appear to be cards with the DIY/LEG badges and price ranges that differ from the FIELD/CERT majority) have a different tag hierarchy. Refactor each card to use identical wrapper tags, heading levels, and spec-row markup so they look and parse uniformly.

On /gallery, give every storm-feature thumbnail/filter glyph the same bounding box and underlying SVG shape conventions. Right now the supercell silhouettes vary in height/aspect across the 12 visible tiles (some are deep classic bells, some are flatter HP wedges, some show mammatus rows or lightning bolts at very different scales). Either standardize the artboard size and stroke weight across all SVG thumbnails, or wrap each thumbnail in a fixed-aspect container so they line up as a coherent grid.

On /calendar, tighten the day-cell alignment. The current grid has 28 alignment errors across 64 elements — most stem from inconsistent vertical placement of the day-number and risk-chip inside cells with different content lengths. Use a consistent flexbox or grid layout per cell (e.g. day number top-left at a fixed offset, risk chip bottom-left at a fixed offset) so the chips and numbers line up across rows regardless of whether the cell carries an MRGL/SLGT/ENH/MOD/HIGH tag.
